originalTitle: Five patients recover from Ebola in DR Congo and leave hospital
title: Happy News! Friends in DR Congo Get Well from Ebola!
content:
Hello, my dear friends! It’s me, your friendly news reporter for Africa! I have some super happy news to share with you today. Are you ready? It’s about people getting better and feeling strong again!

Wonderful News from DR Congo!

Guess what? In a country called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short, something amazing happened. Five people who were sick with something called Ebola are now feeling much, much better! They have recovered! This means they are strong and healthy again. They even left the hospital! A hospital is a special place where doctors and nurses help sick people get well. Imagine waving goodbye to your friends when they are happy and smiling again! That's what happened for these five people.

What is Ebola? (Super Simple!)

Now, you might be wondering, "What is Ebola?" It's like a tiny, tiny germ, so small you can't even see it without a special magnifying machine! These tiny germs can make people feel very sick. **Did you know?** Germs are everywhere! Most germs are harmless, but some can make us cough or feel unwell. Ebola is a serious germ. It can give people a fever, which means their body gets very hot. It can also make their tummy hurt, or make them feel very, very tired.

How Do People Get Better?

The good news is that people *can* get better from Ebola! Just like when you have a cold and then you feel better after a few days, people can also recover from Ebola. But it's super important to get help fast. When someone feels sick, especially with something like Ebola, they need to go to a special place where doctors and nurses can take care of them. This place is the hospital! **Did you know?** Doctors and nurses are like superheroes! They work very hard to help people feel better and stay healthy. At the hospital, doctors and nurses give people special medicines and take good care of them. They make sure people have enough to drink and eat. They watch over them day and night.

Why is it Important to Tell an Adult?

If you ever feel sick, it's super, super important to tell a grown-up right away. Tell your mom, your dad, your grandma, your teacher, or any adult you trust. They can help you decide what to do. Maybe you just need a rest, or maybe you need to see a doctor. It’s always best to tell someone quickly. Sometimes, when people feel sick with Ebola, they might not know it's Ebola at first. They might just feel tired or have a fever. That's why telling an adult and going to a doctor is so important. Doctors have special ways to check what kind of germ is making someone sick.

Who Helps People Get Well?

There are many amazing people who help others recover from sickness. We call them health workers. They are doctors, nurses, and people who help clean hospitals or bring food to patients. They are all very brave and kind. In DR Congo, these health workers are doing an amazing job. They work hard every day to help people feel better and stop the spread of germs. **Did you know?** There are health workers all over Africa, helping people in villages and big cities! They are true helpers in our communities.

What Can We Do to Stay Safe?

Even though Ebola is a serious germ, there are simple things we can all do to help stay safe and healthy. 1. **Wash your hands!** This is number one! Wash your hands with soap and clean water many times a day. Especially after playing, before eating, and after going to the toilet. Washing hands helps to wash away tiny germs. **Did you know?** Washing your hands for as long as it takes to sing "Happy Birthday" twice is a good way to get them really clean! 2. **Don't touch your face!** Try not to touch your eyes, nose, or mouth with unwashed hands. Germs can get into your body easily this way. 3. **Stay a little bit away from people who are very sick.** This is just to be extra careful. If someone is coughing or sneezing a lot, give them a little space. 4. **Eat healthy food and drink clean water.** This helps your body stay strong and ready to fight off any germs!

More Good News: People Get Better!

The most important message from this happy news in DR Congo is this: If someone gets sick, they *can* get better! The special people who help us, like doctors and nurses, say it clearly: if someone has symptoms (that means they feel sick in some way, like a fever or feeling tired), they should ask for medical help. **Did you know?** Medical help just means getting help from doctors or nurses! They know a lot about how to make people healthy again. Seeking medical help quickly means doctors can start helping sooner. And the sooner they help, the better chance someone has to recover completely, just like our five friends in DR Congo!

A Big Cheer for DR Congo!

Let's give a big cheer for DR Congo and for all the brave doctors, nurses, and health workers who are helping people every day. And let's give an even bigger cheer for the five people who are now happy and healthy again! This news reminds us that even when things are tough, there is always hope, and people can get well. Let’s all stay safe, be kind to each other, and remember to wash our hands! That’s all for today’s happy news from your African reporter! See you next time!
summary: Five people who were sick with Ebola in DR Congo are now well and healthy, showing that people can recover if they get medical help quickly.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows everyone that even serious sicknesses like Ebola can be beaten, and getting help from doctors and nurses early can make people healthy again.

originalTitle: Voting suspended in parts of Ethiopia over security concerns
title: Hello from Ethiopia: Learning About Choices!
content:
Hello, Wonderful Friends!

Hi everyone, it's your friendly news reporter for Africa! Today, we're going on a special journey to a beautiful country called Ethiopia. It's a land of sunshine, amazing history, and super friendly people. Sometimes, in countries all over the world, people get to choose their leaders. It's like choosing a captain for your sports team or a class president for your school. This special day is called an election! It's a very exciting time because it means everyone gets to have a say in who helps run their country. People go to a special place to mark their choice on a paper. It's a bit like a big quiz, but for choosing who will lead.

What is an Election?

Imagine you have a big cake, and everyone wants to choose who gets to cut the slices. In a country, people need someone to help make important decisions. They choose someone who they think will do a good job. This choosing time is called an election. Grown-ups go to a place called a polling station. There, they get a special paper with names of people or "teams" who want to lead. Then, they put a mark next to the person or team they like best. It's a secret choice, so nobody knows what you picked! This way, everyone feels safe to choose freely. Did you know? The oldest human footprints were found in Africa! It shows how long people have lived here!

Voting in Ethiopia!

In Ethiopia, people were also getting ready for their election day. They were excited to choose their leaders. But something happened that made things a little tricky in some places. Ethiopia is a big country with many different towns and villages. In some of these places, the voting had to wait. It was like pausing a game to make sure everyone is ready and safe. This is because the grown-ups in charge wanted to make sure everyone was super safe and happy when they voted. Safety is super important, right? Did you know? Ethiopia is famous for being the birthplace of coffee! So next time you smell coffee, think of Ethiopia!

Why Did Some Voting Stop?

You might be wondering, "Why did the voting stop in some places?" Well, sometimes, when many people have different ideas, there can be some disagreements. These are called "conflicts" or "security concerns." It just means that the leaders wanted to make sure everything was calm and peaceful for everyone. They didn't want anyone to feel worried or unsafe while they were trying to make their important choice. So, in a few parts of Ethiopia, they decided to wait. They want to make sure that when voting happens again, everyone can go to the polling station feeling happy and secure. It's just like when your teacher might pause a fun game if things get a bit too noisy or rough, to make sure everyone is having fun safely.

Who is Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed?

Every country needs a main leader. In Ethiopia, that leader is called the Prime Minister. His name is Abiy Ahmed. He is like the head coach of the whole country! Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed leads a team of people. This team is sometimes called a "party." They all work together and have similar ideas about how to make Ethiopia a great place. Many people in Ethiopia like Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's team. They think his team will do a great job. So, a lot of people expect that his team might win many of the votes. He and his team want to help Ethiopia grow and make life better for everyone. They think about schools, roads, hospitals, and how to keep everyone safe. Did you know? Ethiopia has its own unique calendar, which is seven years behind the Western calendar! How cool is that?

What Happens Next?

So, what happens now that voting has paused in some areas? The people in charge are working hard to make sure everything is calm and safe. They want to fix any disagreements. Once everything is super peaceful and ready, the voting will happen again in those places. Everyone will get their chance to choose their leaders, just like in the other parts of Ethiopia. It's like waiting for the rain to stop before you can go out and play. You wait until it's safe and sunny again! This means that everyone will still get to have their say. It's important that every voice is heard, no matter what.

Did You Know? Fun Facts About Ethiopia!

Let's learn some more amazing things about Ethiopia! * Did you know? Ethiopia is the only African country that was never colonized by European powers. That means they've always been independent and governed themselves! * Did you know? There are over 80 different languages spoken in Ethiopia! Imagine hearing so many different sounds! * Did you know? Ethiopia has beautiful mountains, including Ras Dashen, one of the highest peaks in Africa. It's like a giant's playground! * Did you know? Ethiopian food is super yummy! They have a special bread called Injera, which is spongy and delicious, perfect for scooping up stews.

Why is This Important?

Even though the voting stopped in some places, it’s important to remember that leaders want everyone to be safe. It shows that grown-ups are thinking about how to keep peace. Choosing leaders is a very big job, and it’s important that it happens in a calm way. Soon, all of Ethiopia will have chosen their leaders, and they will work together to make the country even better! We send lots of happy thoughts to our friends in Ethiopia. We hope everything will be calm and peaceful very soon so everyone can make their important choices!
summary: Voting in some parts of Ethiopia paused to make sure everyone stays safe, as people get ready to choose their leaders like Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's team.
whyMatters: It matters because choosing leaders is how countries decide who will help make important decisions, and it's super important that everyone is safe and happy when they make their choice.

originalTitle: UK wins court case over collapsed Rwanda asylum deal
title: UK wins court case over collapsed Rwanda asylum deal
content: The UK does not need to pay Rwanda after it pulled a deal between the two countries, a court has ruled.

originalTitle: US Defense Department bars journalists from its press office
title: US Defense Department bars journalists from its press office
content: Media freedom advocates condemn move as latest effort to curtail independent reporting on the US military.

originalTitle: President Trump attempts to end hostilities in Lebanon
title: Good News! Leaders Talk for Peace!
content:
Hello, amazing kids of Africa! Your friendly news reporter here, bringing you some happy news from far away. Today, we have a story about leaders who are working hard to make our world a safer and more peaceful place for everyone, especially for awesome kids like you! It’s all about talking, not fighting, which is super important.

Talking for Peace!

We have some really good news today! A very important man, President Trump, from a big country called America, is helping to make peace. He wants to stop worries and arguments between countries. It’s like when you and your friend have a little disagreement, and then you both decide to talk it out and be friends again. That’s what grown-up leaders try to do too!

Who is President Trump?

President Trump is like the head teacher of a very, very big school, but for a whole country! He is the leader of America. America is a huge country that is very far from us, across a big ocean. His job is to look after all the people in America and try to help other countries too. Did you know? America has tall buildings called skyscrapers and famous places like Disney World where cartoon characters live!

What Does 'Stopping Worries' Mean?

Sometimes, countries have big arguments. When countries argue a lot, it can make people worried and scared. This is what we mean by 'hostilities' – it means things are not calm. No one likes fighting, especially not kids or their families! We all want to live in peace.

Talking to Another Leader

President Trump did something very important. He talked to another very important leader! This leader is called the Prime Minister of Israel. Let’s call him Mr. PM for short. Israel is another country, not too far from our big continent, Africa. It’s a place with a lot of history and special stories. Did you know? Israel is famous for its ancient cities and sunny beaches!

Where is Lebanon?

These two leaders were talking about a country called Lebanon. In Lebanon, there is a very big and beautiful city named Beirut. Imagine the biggest city you know in Africa, with lots of people, shops, and busy streets. Beirut is just like that for Lebanon! It's a lively place where people live, work, and play. Did you know? Lebanon has amazing food, like hummus and falafel, and even beautiful snowy mountains!

What is 'Diplomacy'?

This is a big word, but it means something really simple and good! 'Diplomacy' means when important grown-ups from different countries talk to each other. They don't shout or fight. They talk carefully to solve problems and make things better for everyone. It’s all about finding smart ways to be friends, even when things are difficult. Think of it as grown-ups using their words to build bridges instead of walls between countries.

Stopping Things from Getting Worse

President Trump and Mr. PM talked to 'halt escalation.' This means they want to stop things from getting more difficult or dangerous for the people in Lebanon, especially in Beirut. They want to make sure that any small argument doesn't grow into a big problem. They want to keep everyone safe. It's like when you see two friends getting ready to argue, and a grown-up steps in to help them calm down before it gets too loud. That’s what these leaders are trying to do for countries.

Why is Peace Important?

When there is peace, wonderful things happen! Kids can go to school safely every day. Families can play together in parks and enjoy their homes without worries. Everyone can live without fear, which means more smiles and more laughter for everyone. Peace means quiet and calm, where people can learn, grow, and be happy together. It helps countries become stronger and happier. Did you know? Many countries around the world have a special 'Peace Day' where everyone celebrates being kind and friendly!

Good News for Everyone

So, President Trump talked to the other leader, and they both want to make sure that the people in Lebanon, especially in the big city of Beirut, are safe and sound. This is truly good news for everyone around the world! It shows that talking is always, always better than fighting. When leaders talk, they can help keep kids and families safe, which is the most important thing. It reminds us that even when things are tricky, grown-ups are working hard to bring peace.

What Can We Do?

Even awesome kids like you can help make peace right here in Africa! How? By being kind to your friends at school. By sharing your toys with your siblings. By helping your family at home. And by always using your words to solve problems instead of getting angry. These are all small ways we can spread peace and kindness every single day. A smile, a shared snack, or a helping hand can make a big difference. Did you know? Sharing a friendly smile with someone can make them feel happy and spread kindness to others! That's all for today's good news, fantastic kids of Africa! Remember, peace is super important, and we all have a part to play in making the world a kinder place. Keep smiling, keep learning, and keep spreading kindness wherever you go!
summary: US President Trump talked with Israel's leader to help stop arguments and keep peace in the city of Beirut, Lebanon, showing that talking is better than fighting.
whyMatters: This is important because when leaders talk for peace, it helps keep kids and families safe, allows people to go to school, and makes sure everyone can live without fear in their homes.

originalTitle: Celebration, shock and scepticism follow Colombia’s presidential election
title: Big Surprise! A New Leader for Colombia!
content:
Hello from Your African News Reporter!

Hi, little explorers! It's me, your friendly news reporter from Africa. I'm here to tell you about some exciting news from a country very far away. It's a country called Colombia! Colombia is not in Africa. It's in a place called South America. It's a land of amazing rainforests and tall mountains. Did you know that Colombia is famous for its beautiful flowers and delicious coffee? It's also home to lots of amazing animals, like colourful parrots and speedy jaguars!

What is an Election?

Every now and then, big countries have something called an "election." Think of it like choosing a captain for your sports team or a class monitor. When grown-ups have an election, they get to choose a new "leader" for their whole country. This leader is often called a "President." The President's job is super important! They help make decisions to keep everyone safe and happy. They want to make sure kids can go to school and that people have good homes.

Choosing a New President in Colombia

Recently, the grown-ups in Colombia had an election. They were voting to choose who would be their next President. It's a really big deal for a country to choose its leader. Everyone wants the best person for the job! Did you know that in many countries, people use special papers called ballots to vote? They mark their choice, and then all the votes are counted carefully.

Meet the Two Main People

In this election, there were two main people who wanted to be President. They were like two different teams. One person was called Abelardo de la Espriella. He is a lawyer, which means he helps people understand rules and laws. The other person was called Ivan Cepeda. He is a "Senator," which means he also helps make rules for the country, just in a different way. Think of them like two very smart people who have different ideas about how to make their country even better.

Different Ideas for the Country

Sometimes, people have different ideas about how to run things. It's like how you might want to play football, but your friend wants to play hide-and-seek. Both are fun, but they are different! Abelardo de la Espriella is called "far-right." This is a grown-up word. It just means he has certain ideas about how the government should work. Maybe he thinks the government should let people make more choices by themselves. Ivan Cepeda is called "left-wing." This is another grown-up word. It means he has different ideas. Maybe he thinks the government should help people a lot more, like making sure everyone has access to good schools and hospitals. It's okay to have different ideas! That's how countries talk about what's best.

The First Round of Voting

In some countries, it takes a few tries to choose a President. This election in Colombia had a "first round." Imagine you are playing a game, and you need to get a certain number of points to win. If nobody gets enough points in the first try, you play another round! That's a bit like what happened in Colombia. Everyone voted, and then they counted all the votes.

A Big Surprise!

And guess what? There was a big surprise! Many people thought one person would win this first round easily. But it didn't happen that way! Abelardo de la Espriella won more votes than Ivan Cepeda in the first try. This was a big shock for many people! They were not expecting Abelardo to get so many votes right away. Did you know that "upending expectations" means that something happened that was very different from what most people thought would happen? It's like expecting rain, but then the sun shines all day!

Feelings After the Vote

When big news like this happens, people have lots of feelings! Some people were very happy. These were Abelardo's friends and supporters. They celebrated because their chosen leader did very well. They probably cheered and clapped, happy that their team was winning! Other people were very surprised, or even a little sad. These were Ivan's friends and supporters. They probably thought Ivan would win easily. It's normal to feel different ways after an election. Everyone cares about their country!

What Does "Scepticism" Mean?

The news also mentioned "scepticism." This is a tricky word for grown-ups, so let's make it simple. Scepticism is like when you hear a story, and you think, "Hmm, I wonder if that's really true?" or "What will happen next? Will it be good?" Some people in Colombia felt a bit like this. They were thinking about what Abelardo's win might mean for the future of their country. They might have questions about how things will change or stay the same. It just means they are thinking deeply about the results.

What Happens Next for Colombia?

So, what happens now that Abelardo won the first round? Well, the election isn't over yet! Because nobody got enough votes to win completely in the first round, there will be a "second round" of voting. It's like a final game! Abelardo and Ivan will try again to get the most votes. Soon, the people of Colombia will vote one more time. Then, they will finally choose their new President.

Why This News Matters

This news from Colombia is important because it shows how people in different countries get to choose their leaders. It shows that sometimes, surprising things can happen when people vote. Everyone's vote counts! It also shows that it's good to talk about different ideas for how to make a country great. Did you know that elections are a very important part of democracy? Democracy means that the people get to choose who leads them, which is a wonderful thing! We wish the people of Colombia good luck as they choose their next President. It's a big step for their country!
summary: In a big surprise, Abelardo de la Espriella won more votes than Ivan Cepeda in the first round of the presidential election in faraway Colombia, making many people happy and others quite shocked.
whyMatters: This news shows how exciting elections can be and that every vote counts when people choose their country's leader, even if the results are a big surprise!
